replies.text:
My last fast food was 2019, McDonalds. Only stopped because I was at the end of a road trip and knew I didn't have much at home (since I was gone a couple weeks). It was mostly tasteless and expensive even then. I cook at home 99% of the time now, real food not processed crap. When I do eat out it's at a local mom 'n pop mexican restaurant, a local diner that serves real food, or once in a great while at a local bar and grill.

Cheaper, better food, healthier. Win, Win, Win.

replies.text: If you get diarrhea from them, something is wrong. If you’ve ever worked there, you’d have a different opinion. All employees are trained in cleanliness, sanitation and everything is set up with cross-contamination prevention in mind. Their standards are actually very high compared to any local place you can find. I never saw any food labeled with any crazy ingredients. It’s all just frozen. The Gordon’s “filet o fish” patties are the same sh*t you can buy at Kroger.

author: @McMillanScottish
text: Between the ages of 15 and 50, (when Covid all but destroyed my pro audio career in 2020), I averaged about $24K-$28K/year, with a couple years at 30K.  I mostly lived alone, occasionally sharing a place with a useless girlfriend or two. I managed to get by, pay rent, save money, feed myself, and be entertained for that much. And that was living just outside Chicago. So after Trump took office the first time and rocketed my IRA to the stars, I cashed out and moved to the middle of a red southern state, bought 3 acres with a house and a few buildings on it, paid cash, paid off all my cars, and now I live on about $1200 a month and I still save money.. People gotta figure out how to get through this world man. Everybody is spending way too much money.

author: @meh_lady
text: IDK why people waste their money on restaurant food unless it's a necessity. Maybe it's because we grew up with home cooking as the default and I kept doing what I knew. An $8 burger from McD's blows my mind.

text:

A lot of that is caused by greed and upper managers screwing us so other people can keep their 40+ per week but the fact is this same department was working 48 hours/week minimum before Biden. That was 8 hours overtime for everyone, every week

replies.author: @BB-ed4om
replies.text: Everything is poison. Do you do this with alcohol, weed, sugary drinks, pesticides, dyes, hormones, etc? Or just crusade against one restaurant? It’s always just “mcdonalds” is the villain. Over-consuming anything is poisonous. It’s supposed to be once in  a great while food. When I worked there I saw all of the food packaging. I never saw anything offensive or shocking. It’s just mostly frozen. They actually have very high standards for cleanliness and train their employees on cross contamination, etc. compared to local diners and coney islands McDonald’s has way better standards.

replies.author: @dc85
replies.text: ​@BB-ed4om fast food in general, and restaurants aren't far behind.  Not saying it's an option for everyone, but we source our food direct from farm to butcher.  It isn't just the food itself, it's the people cooking it also, no thanks.

author: @Droneguysnj
text:
One major thing changed that hasn't really been mentioned. Workers salaries went crazy at McDonald's. My friend's kid works there and gets $17 an hour. That was a $7.50 an hour job in 2019. Messed up times.

This is all part of the plan.

author: @ians2777
text: The add one for a dollar might be a bit weird but getting rid of it means yet ANOTHER price increase from them and they have the audacity to try to claim they are doing it to increase value. There's 4 things on my local "add" menu- $2.50 fry, $3 6-pc nugget, $3 mcchicken, $3.70 dbl cheeseburger. That meant 12 nuggets for $4 instead of 10 for $5.40, or $4 for 2 mcchickens instead of $6, or 2 burgers for $4.70 instead of $7.40. Even 2 mcdoubles would be $7 now. All of these items used to cost $1 each incase anyone forgot. So I'm not expecting whatever they change prices to will be reasonable.

author: @daviddaniels9551
text:
I was lucky to work for a company that had a match+ program.  I had the funds taken out before I ever saw them, so I didn't have a way to complain or gripe.  Later, I put together a second program, one I tossed a few bucks into whenever I felt comfortable, like putting away part of a bonus or reward.
Thank God!!!
Because now that I'm retired, I see how little Social Security gets you.  I honestly don't see how some people get by, especially inn states like California, where they are taxing Everything.  I think they tax the tax!

author: @The_Lucent_Archangel
text: Remember also that if you used a delivery app prior to the Coof, it was maybe 3-4 bucks on top of the food price and so people were more keen on giving a tip. Then they bumped the fees way up and we started getting drivers straight up leaving untipped orders for last if they got picked up at all because they apparently didn't make the connection that their little side hustle had gotten way too expensive for people to throw them a couple bucks on top of the food and delivery fee. And none of that has gone down, along with those companies apparently just not caring if the drivers delivering food actually match the people they hired.
